title: 여기가 옛날엔 바다였거든
title_en: This Used to Be the Sea

goal.ko: -거든요로 배경 설명을, -잖아요로 공유된 사실을 이야기할 수 있어요.
goal.en: I can use -거든요 to give background and -잖아요 to refer to shared knowledge.

key_points: -거든요 — gives background or reason after what you just said (you see / the thing is)
-잖아요 (심화) — reminds listener of something they already know (as you know / remember?)
-았/었으면 좋겠다 — expressing a hope or wish

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 와! 바다 위를 달리는 것 같아요!
script.en: Wow! It feels like we're driving on top of the sea!

script.speaker: grandma
script.en: This used to be the sea, you see.

annotation.note:
거든요 = providing background context
여기가 옛날엔 바다였거든
= This used to be the sea, you know
= 상대가 모를 것 같아 설명해주는 뉘앙스

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 이 방조제로 물을 가두고 바다를 메우고 있잖아.
script.en: This seawall holds back the water and they're filling in the sea, you know.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 이렇게 우여곡절이 많은 공사도 없을 거야.
script.en: There's probably no other project that's had so many twists and turns.

annotation.note:
네요 = expressing new realization or feeling
안타깝네요 = That's a shame (I feel it now)
= 새로운 정보에 대한 감탄·반응
예쁘네요! / 맛있네요! / 그렇네요!

script.speaker: grandma
script.en: It seems like they've finally found the right direction.

script.speaker: mirae
script.en: How did they find their direction?

script.speaker: grandma
script.text: 모든 에너지가 자급자족되는 스마트 신도시를 건설한다고 하네.
script.en: Apparently they're going to build a smart new city that generates all its own energy.

script.speaker: mirae
script.en: I hope it goes well this time.

annotation.grammar: V-았/었으면 좋겠어요 — I wish / I hope
annotation.note:
V + 았/었으면 좋겠어요 = I hope / I wish
잘 되었으면 좋겠어요 = I hope it goes well
비가 안 왔으면 좋겠어요 = I wish it wouldn't rain
= 바람·희망 표현

script.speaker: grandpa
script.text: 그러게나 말이다. 할머니 할아버지가 여기에 처음 와 보고 홀딱 반했거든.
script.en: Exactly. Your grandma and I completely fell for this place the first time we came, you see.

script.speaker: grandma
script.en: The natural scenery was just so beautiful, as you can see.

script.speaker: grandpa
script.en: And Jeolla-do is famous for its food, you know.

script.speaker: lia
script.text: 그럼 여기서 맛있는 점심을 먹고 가야겠어요!
script.en: Then we should definitely have a delicious lunch here before we go!

items.english: seawall — a barrier built to hold back seawater
to contain / to hold back / to confine
to fill in / to reclaim (land from sea)
self-sufficiency — producing what you need yourself · 자급자족되다 = to be self-sufficient
twists and turns / complicated ups and downs · 우여곡절이 많다 = to have a troubled history
to find one's direction / to get on track
outside · 밖을 내다보다 = to look outside
to feel sorry / to be a shame · 안타깝네요 = what a shame
to completely fall for / to be swept away (by a place, person, or thing)
old times / the past · 옛날엔 = back in the day
hometown of taste / land famous for its food · 고장 = hometown / birthplace

grammar.title: -거든요
grammar.explanation_en: Use -거든요 to give background information or explain the reason for what you just said. It comes AFTER the main statement — like saying 'you see' or 'the thing is' at the end.
grammar.pattern: Verb/Adj stem + -거든(요)  ·  N + 이거든(요) [consonant ending] / 거든(요) [vowel ending]

scene_example.english: This used to be the sea, you see. / We completely fell for it, you see.

rule_boxes.title: Statement first, then -거든요 · 순서
rule_boxes.content:
Statement → 거든요 (explanation follows)
'왜 우산 가져가?' → '비가 오거든.' (It's raining, you see.)
'왜 안 먹어?' → '배가 불거든.' (I'm full, that's why.)

rule_boxes.title: Formal vs informal · 거든 vs 거든요
rule_boxes.content:
-거든 = informal (friends, family, kids)
-거든요 = polite (teachers, elders, strangers)
Ep11: 할머니 uses 거든 with grandkids
In adult conversation: 거든요

rule_boxes.title: -거든요 vs -니까 · 핵심 차이
rule_boxes.content:
-니까 = reason BEFORE the result
비가 오니까 우산 가져가. (Because it's raining, take an umbrella.)
-거든요 = background AFTER the statement
우산 가져가. 비가 오거든. (Take an umbrella. It's raining, you see.)

examples.ko: 나 늦을 것 같아. 차가 막히거든.
examples.en: I might be late. Traffic is bad, you see.
I like Korean food. It's delicious, you see.
I haven't seen that movie. It's scary, you see.

grammar.title: -잖아요 (심화)
grammar.explanation_en: Use -잖아요 to remind your listener of something both of you already know. It can also gently correct or add emphasis. The key: both speakers share this knowledge.

scene_example.korean: 바다를 메우고 있잖아. / 전라도는 맛의 고장이잖아.
scene_example.english: They're filling in the sea, you know. / Jeolla-do is famous for its food, you know.

rule_boxes.title: Shared knowledge · 공유 지식
rule_boxes.content:
Both speakers already know this — you're reminding them
자연풍광이 아름다웠잖아요. (The scenery was beautiful, as you saw.)
전라도는 맛의 고장이잖아. (Jeolla-do is famous for food, you know.)

rule_boxes.title: Mild correction or pushback · 살짝 정정
rule_boxes.content:
Can gently push back or correct:
그거 이미 했잖아요! (We already did that, remember?)
내가 먼저 왔잖아! (I got here first, you know!)

rule_boxes.title: -거든요 vs -잖아요 · 핵심 차이
rule_boxes.content:
-거든요 = NEW info to listener (they didn't know this)
'전라도는 맛의 고장이거든요.' ← listener is hearing this for first time

-잖아요 = SHARED info (both already know it)
'전라도는 맛의 고장이잖아요.' ← we both know this, just reminding

examples.en: That restaurant is great, you know. Let's go!
The weather is nice today, you know. Let's go out.
I told you, didn't I — that I'd be late.

grammar.title: -네요
grammar.explanation_en: Use -네요 when you just noticed something or realized something for the first time in that moment. It expresses mild surprise or a new impression.

scene_example.english: That's such a shame (I just feel it now).

rule_boxes.title: Fresh impression · 즉각적인 반응
rule_boxes.content:
Said in the moment of noticing or feeling something:
경치가 정말 아름답네요! (The view is really beautiful!)
생각보다 맵네요. (It's spicier than I thought.)
오늘 날씨 좋네요. (Oh, the weather's nice today.)

rule_boxes.title: Tone range · 뉘앙스
rule_boxes.content:
Mild surprise or fresh realization — NOT strong emotion
✅ 오늘 날씨가 좋네요. (Oh, the weather's nice today.)
✅ 한국어를 잘 하시네요! (Oh, your Korean is great!)
❌ 너무 무서워요! (This is scary!) → 무섭네요 would be calmer

rule_boxes.title: -네요 vs -아/어요 · 차이
rule_boxes.content:
-아/어요: plain fact statement
날씨가 좋아요. (The weather is good.)
-네요: fresh realization, you just noticed
날씨가 좋네요. (Oh, the weather is nice!) ← just stepped outside

examples.en: Oh, you're good at Korean!
This food is really good!
Oh wow, the scenery is truly beautiful!

grammar.title: -았/었으면 좋겠다
grammar.explanation_en: Use this to express a hope or wish about something you want to happen. Milder and more personal than a direct request.

scene_example.english: I hope it goes well this time.

rule_boxes.title: Hoping for something · 바람
rule_boxes.content:
비가 안 왔으면 좋겠어요. (I hope it doesn't rain.)
시험에 붙었으면 좋겠어. (I hope I pass the exam.)
빨리 나았으면 좋겠다. (I hope you get better soon.)

rule_boxes.title: Negative wish · 안 ~으면 좋겠다
rule_boxes.content:
Add 안 before the verb to wish something WON'T happen:
비가 안 왔으면 좋겠어요. (I hope it doesn't rain.)
시험이 안 어려웠으면 좋겠어요. (I hope the exam isn't hard.)

rule_boxes.title: Formality · 격식
rule_boxes.content:
Casual (반말): ~으면 좋겠어
      날씨가 좋았으면 좋겠어.
Polite (존댓말): ~으면 좋겠어요
      이번엔 잘 됐으면 좋겠어요.
Tip: 좋겠다 softens the wish — less demanding than 해야 해요.

examples.en: I hope the weather is nice.
I wish I could go to Korea.
I hope it goes well this time.

grammar.title: -아/어야겠다
grammar.explanation_en: Use this when you've just decided you need to do something — a personal resolution in the moment.

scene_example.korean: 그럼 여기서 맛있는 점심을 먹고 가야겠어요!
scene_example.english: Then we should definitely have a delicious lunch here before we go!

rule_boxes.title: Personal resolve · 결심
rule_boxes.content:
In-the-moment decision to do something:
일찍 자야겠어요. (I should sleep early.)
좀 더 열심히 공부해야겠다. (I'd better study harder.)
이제 가야겠어요. (I think it's time to go.)

rule_boxes.title: -아/어야겠다 vs -아/어야 해요 · 차이
rule_boxes.content:
-아/어야겠다 = self-directed resolve (I've decided I should)
일찍 자야겠어요. (I should sleep early — my own decision)
-아/어야 해요 = external obligation or rule
8시까지 와야 해요. (You have to be here by 8 — it's required)

rule_boxes.title: Trigger context · 언제 써요?
rule_boxes.content:
You realize something and make an in-the-moment decision:
비가 오네. 우산 챙겨야겠다. (Oh it's raining. I should grab an umbrella.)
배가 고프네. 뭔가 먹어야겠다. (I'm hungry. I should eat something.)

examples.en: I should exercise more.
I should read this book again.
I really should memorize this word.

banmal_jondaemal.intro_en: This episode's key contrast: informal -거든 / -잖아 vs polite -거든요 / -잖아요

rows.note: grandma uses 거든 with grandkids — switch to 거든요 with adults

rows.situation_en: explaining a feeling or experience as background

adult_dialogue.setup_en: Two adults talk about the Saemangeum Seawall.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: What kind of place is Saemangeum?

lines.speaker: B
lines.text: 거기가 원래 바다였거든요. 지금은 간척 사업 중이에요.
lines.en: It used to be the sea, you see. They're doing land reclamation there now.

lines.speaker: A
lines.en: Oh I see. The natural scenery there was beautiful, wasn't it?

lines.speaker: B
lines.en: Yes, so there are things to regret as well.

banmal_jondaemal.tip: -거든요 = explaining new info · -잖아요 = reminding of shared info. Mastering this distinction sounds very native!

items.tip:
When ㄱ (받침) is followed by ㅈ, the ㅈ becomes ㅉ (tensified):
곡 + 절 → [곡쩔]
Same pattern: 학교 → [학꾜], 국자 → [국짜]
ㅂ받침 + ㅈ → tensification [ㅉ]: 급+자 → [급짜]. Same pattern: 입장→[입짱], 법정→[법쩡]

quiz.question: What does 할머니 say about this place?
options: It used to be the sea.
It used to be a rice field.
It was a lake long ago.

quiz.audio_text: 모든 에너지가 자급자족되는 스마트 신도시를 건설한다고 하네.
quiz.question: What is the new plan for Saemangeum?

quiz.audio_text: 그러게나 말이다. 할머니 할아버지가 여기에 처음 와 보고 홀딱 반했거든.
quiz.question: What does 할아버지 say about the first visit?
options: He and grandma completely fell in love with this place.
He didn't want to come back.

quiz.question: Which sentence uses -거든요 correctly to give background info?
quiz.explanation: -거든요 comes AFTER the main statement to explain the reason. '많이 먹었거든' explains WHY they're full.

quiz.question: Which sentence uses -잖아요 correctly?
options: 전라도는 맛의 고장이잖아요. (reminding that both know this)
전라도는 맛의 고장이거든요. (telling as new info)
전라도는 맛의 고장인 것 같아요. (it seems like)
quiz.explanation: -잖아요 = reminding of something both speakers already know. Both speakers are aware Jeolla-do is famous for food.

quiz.audio_text: 그럼 여기서 맛있는 점심을 먹고 가야겠어요!
quiz.question: What does 리나 decide to do?
options: Have a delicious lunch here before leaving

real_life.title: 새만금 방조제란?
real_life.passage: 새만금 방조제는 전라북도에 있어요. 세계에서 가장 긴 방조제 중 하나예요. 바닷물을 막아 땅을 만들고 있는 대규모 간척 사업이에요. 공사는 1991년에 시작됐어요. 처음에는 농업용 땅을 만들려고 했는데, 계획이 여러 번 바뀌었어요. 지금은 스마트 신도시와 재생에너지 단지를 건설할 계획이에요.

messages.en: Why do you look so tired today?

messages.text: 어젯밤에 잠을 못 잤거든. 아기가 울었어.
messages.en: I couldn't sleep last night, you see. The baby was crying.
Oh no. But you can have coffee, you know!
I can't have caffeine, you see. My heart races, you see.

self_check: -거든요를 써서 배경 정보를 설명할 수 있어요 · I can use -거든요 to give background
-잖아요를 써서 공유된 사실을 상기시킬 수 있어요 · I can use -잖아요 to remind of shared knowledge
-거든요와 -잖아요의 차이를 알아요 · I know the difference between -거든요 and -잖아요
-네요로 즉각적인 반응을 표현할 수 있어요 · I can express a fresh reaction with -네요
새만금 방조제가 어떤 곳인지 설명할 수 있어요 · I can explain what the Saemangeum Seawall is

usage.heading: -거든요 — 배경 설명 (giving context)
usage.content: Make a statement, then use -거든요 to explain why. The background comes AFTER.

examples.en: I'm tired these days. I've been doing a lot of overtime, you see.
You can go this way. It's a shortcut, you see.
That restaurant isn't great. The service is bad, you see.

usage.heading: -잖아요 — 공유 지식 상기 (reminding shared knowledge)
usage.content: Remind your listener of something both of you already know. Can also be a gentle correction.

examples.ko: 한국 사람들 밥 좋아하잖아요. 밥 먹었어요?
examples.en: Koreans love rice, you know. Have you eaten?

examples.ko: 내가 먼저 왔잖아! 왜 먼저 해요?
examples.en: I got here first, you know! Why are you going first?

examples.ko: 그 노래 엄청 유명하잖아. 모를 수가 없지.
examples.en: That song is super famous, you know. There's no way you don't know it.

usage.title: 💡 핵심 차이
usage.content_ko:
-거든요 = 상대방이 모르는 새 정보 · '새만금은 원래 바다였거든요.' (you didn't know this)
-잖아요 = 상대방도 아는 공유 정보 · '전라도는 맛의 고장이잖아요.' (we both know this)
usage.content_en: 거든요 = new info for the listener · 잖아요 = reminding of shared knowledge
